title: Gate
description: A single gate's outcome inside the gauntlet. Gates run in a fixed binding order; the first FAIL is the binding gate. PASS = the gate's standard was met. FAIL = the standard was not met. SKIP = the gate was not applicable / inputs absent (counts as neither pass nor fail). ADVISORY = the gate ran and is reported but is non-binding (e.g. a candidate-vs-zero PBO is structurally unfailable, so it is advisory and never counted as a confident PASS).

id.description: The canonical gate id, one of the eight committed gauntlet gates.

reason.description: One-line human reason: why it passed, what killed it, or why it was skipped / advisory.

binding.description: True if this gate is the binding constraint (the first FAIL). At most one gate in a verdict is binding.

detail.description: Gate-specific numeric/string/boolean evidence (e.g. dsrProbability, pboValue, placeboP, surr95). Values are scalars for an honest, machine-checkable evidence record.
